machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (OM ring of basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: The OM ring component EscC is essential for T3 secretion and needle complex formation... EscC belongs to the secretin family of proteins that function as channels for secretion of bacterial proteins across the OM in several secretion systems.
machinery_genes.note: A/E-pathogen T3SS review (EPEC/EHEC/C. rodentium). EscC = SctC secretin; also named directly as a LEE 'type III secretion apparatus' ORF (escC, L0041) in the O157:H7 EDL933 LEE sequence (Perna 1998, PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring of basal body (outer IM ring)
machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: SctD/PrgH/YscD family inner MS-ring. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escD, L0024) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: SctJ/PrgK/YscJ-family lipoprotein inner ring; EscJ self-assembles into a periplasmic ring. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escJ, L0039) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: It is presumed that the OM and IM rings are connected through a periplasmic inner rod formed by the oligomerization of the EscI protein
machinery_genes.note: SctI/PrgJ/YscI-family inner rod connecting OM and IM rings.

machinery_genes.quote: The needle is a superhelical hollow structure comprised of multiple copies of the EscF protein (Wilson et al.,), which is essential for the secretion of all T3 substrates and hence for virulence
machinery_genes.note: SctF/PrgI/YscF-family needle. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escF, L0018) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV; Deng et al.,; Diepold and Wagner,; Portaliou et al.,).
machinery_genes.note: SctR/SpaP/YscR export-gate component. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escR, L0049) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV; Deng et al.,; Diepold and Wagner,; Portaliou et al.,).
machinery_genes.note: SctS/SpaQ/YscS export-gate component. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escS, L0048) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV; Deng et al.,; Diepold and Wagner,; Portaliou et al.,).
machinery_genes.note: SctT/SpaR/YscT export-gate component. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escT, L0047) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: EscU belongs to the YscU/FlhB family of proteins, whose members are predicted to have two major domains connected through a conserved flexible linker
machinery_genes.note: SctU/SpaS/YscU autoprotease export-gate switch. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escU, L0046) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: EscV, the major constituent of the T3 export apparatus, is also composed of two main domains: an N-terminal region with eight transmembrane helices and a C-terminal cytoplasmic domain
machinery_genes.note: SctV/InvA/YscV/MxiA-family major export-apparatus ring. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escV, L0035) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins (Figure), all of which are important for T3SS function... EscN is a peripheral membrane protein located at the base of the needle complex that energizes the secretion process
machinery_genes.note: SctN/InvC/YscN-family ATPase. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (escN, L0034) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stalk/stator (C-ring link; ATPase negative regulator)
machinery_genes.quote: EscN interacts with EscL, a negative regulator that inhibits its ATPase activity, and with EscO, which conversely, stimulates EscN ATPase activity
machinery_genes.note: SctL/OrgB/YscL/FliH-family stator linking ATPase to C-ring; also implicated as a sorting-platform component.

machinery_genes.quote: functional data suggest that EscO is the evolutionary counterpart of the flagellar FliJ protein, which in turn has been related to the γ subunit of the F1-ATPase
machinery_genes.note: SctO/InvI/YscO/FliJ-family central stalk of the ATPase complex (formerly EscA). EscO stimulates EscN ATPase activity.

machinery_genes.quote: the C-ring is proposed to be a cytoplasmic annular structure located at the base of the basal body, formed mainly by the EscQ protein (formerly known as SepQ), which belongs to the YscQ/FliN (SctQ) protein family
machinery_genes.note: SctQ/SpaO/YscQ C-ring; EscQ = formerly SepQ. Named directly as a LEE apparatus ORF (sepQ, L0031) in Perna 1998 (PMID 9673266).

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens the sorting platform might be formed by EscQ, EscL, and EscK (formerly Orf4), a component that has been associated with the SctK protein family... Like other members of the SctK protein family such as OrgA from and MxiK from, EscK is critical for T3 secretion
machinery_genes.note: SctK/OrgA/YscK-family C-ring base / sorting-platform component (formerly Orf4).

machinery_genes.quote: In A/E pathogens, the EscP protein belongs to the T3S4 protein family, and in EPEC it was demonstrated to directly interact with EscF
machinery_genes.note: SctP/InvJ/YscP-family molecular ruler controlling needle length; also called a T3S4 substrate-specificity switch protein. Structural/regulatory apparatus component, not a secreted effector.

machinery_genes.quote: It comprises proteins like SepL from A/E pathogens... InvE and SsaL from SPI-1 and SPI-2, respectively... YopN/TyeA from... In A/E pathogens, elimination of SepL abolishes secretion of the translocators and increases secretion of effectors
machinery_genes.note: SctW gatekeeper (InvE/YopN+TyeA/MxiC/SepL family per apparatus brief). SepL interacts with the export gate EscV. Apparatus/gatekeeper, not an effector.

machinery_genes.role: gatekeeper partner of SepL (molecular switch component)
machinery_genes.quote: In EPEC and EHEC SepL interacts with SepD, whose deletion in A/E pathogens results in the same phenotype as the null mutant
machinery_genes.note: SepD forms the gatekeeper switch complex with SepL; same secretion phenotype on deletion. Apparatus-associated regulator of the secretion switch, A/E-pathogen-specific partner of the SctW gatekeeper. Lower-confidence as a 'core' Sct component (no direct Sct family assignment); included because it is an integral part of the SepL gatekeeper switch.

machinery_genes.role: peptidoglycan-lytic enzyme (specialized LEE-encoded PG lytic transglycosylase for apparatus assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: This enzyme has been shown to interact directly with the inner rod subunit EscI (Creasey et al.,; Burkinshaw et al.,). Moreover, the enzymatic activity of EtgA is enhanced in the presence of EscI
machinery_genes.note: LEE-encoded T3SS-associated lytic transglycosylase that locally degrades peptidoglycan to allow injectisome assembly; interacts with the inner rod EscI. Apparatus assembly factor, not a secreted effector.

machinery_genes.role: filament subunit (hollow extracellular conduit between needle and translocon)
machinery_genes.quote: In A/E pathogens, it is assembled by the polymerization of multiple subunits of the EspA protein... forming a hollow structure of 12 nm in width that allows the passage of substrates through a 2.5 nm central channel
machinery_genes.note: A/E-pathogen-specific EspA filament assembled on top of the needle; a structural conduit, not an effector. Distinctive feature of the EPEC/EHEC/LEE injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits.
machinery_genes.note: Hydrophobic translocator forming the host-membrane pore (SipB/IpaB/YopB homolog). Per apparatus brief, translocon proteins are apparatus, not effectors, despite being secreted.

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits.
machinery_genes.note: Hydrophobic translocator of the translocon (SipC/IpaC/YopD homolog). EspB is also translocated into host cells in small amounts, but its structural role is as a pore component, so it is counted as apparatus per the brief. Note: this gene (espB) is ALSO in this instance's curated effector list region; here it is listed only for its translocon/apparatus role.

notes: The LEE (Locus of Enterocyte Effacement) is the ~43 kb pathogenicity island encoding the A/E-pathogen injectisome (T3SS) of EPEC/EHEC, including E. coli O157:H7. The apparatus paper used directly on this serovar is Perna et al. 1998 (Infect Immun), the complete 43,359-bp LEE sequence of O157:H7 EDL933, which is the same O157:H7 LEE as strain Sakai (the two O157:H7 LEEs are >99% identical; only the Esc/Sep apparatus nomenclature post-dates the 1998 sequencing papers). Elliott et al. 1998 sequenced the homologous EPEC E2348/69 LEE. The verbatim per-gene naming/role quotes are taken from the Gaytán et al. 2016 Frontiers review of the A/E-pathogen T3SS, which describes the EPEC/EHEC/LEE injectisome gene-by-gene under the unified Sct nomenclature. Strain transfer: review covers EPEC/EHEC/C. rodentium A/E pathogens collectively; the LEE T3SS is conserved across these, so component identity transfers to O157:H7 Sakai. Core apparatus covered: OM secretin (EscC), IM rings (EscD, EscJ), inner rod (EscI), needle (EscF), export apparatus (EscR/S/T/U/V), ATPase complex (EscN/L/O), C-ring/sorting platform (EscQ/EscK), needle-length ruler EscP, gatekeeper switch (SepL/SepD), PG-lytic transglycosylase EtgA, A/E-specific filament EspA, and translocon (EspD, EspB). Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: LEE-encoded effectors Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ (and EspB's effector role); the adhesin intimin (Eae) and its receptor Tir; chaperones CesAB, CesD, CesD2, CesT, CesF, CesL, EscE, EscG/CesA2; transcriptional regulators Ler, GrlA, GrlR. Lower-confidence calls: SepD (gatekeeper partner without a clean Sct family assignment) and EspB (dual translocon/effector role; counted as apparatus per the brief's rule that translocon proteins are machinery). EscK and EscO C-ring/stalk assignments rest on the review's homology-based statements. No pilotin exists in the A/E-pathogen T3SS (the review explicitly notes its absence and the missing pilotin-binding domain in EscC). All quotes verbatim from the cited PMC full text (Gaytán 2016, PMC5073101).
